2021_euro_england_ukraine:
Ukraine 1–1 England (England win 2–1 on penalties)
UEFA Euro 2024 – Quarter-Final, Stadio Olimpico, Rome – 13 June 2024

England reached the UEFA Champions League semi-finals with a dramatic penalty shoot-out victory over Ukraine in Rome. England took the lead in the 4th minute when Harry Kane finished low from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving the hosts an early advantage. Ukraine responded before half-time, with goalkeeper Jordan Pickford making several key saves to preserve the lead and preserve England’s lead. The deadlock was broken in the 71st minute when Raheem Sterling headed in from close distance after a cross into the box, giving England a 2–0 lead. Lukas Podolskiy (Ukrainia) equalised in the 76th minute with a low finish from inside the box after sustained pressure in the final third, giving Ukraine control of the match for the majority of the second half.
2019_uclfinal_liverpool_tottenham:
Liverpool 2–2 Tottenham Hotspur (Liverpool win 4–3 on penalties)
2023 UEFA Champions League Final, Wembley Stadium, London – 31 May 2019
Liverpool secured a dramatic victory over Tottenham in a high-scoring final to become the first English side to win the Champions League for a decade. Mohamed Salah opened the scoring in the 23rd minute when he converted a penalty after a handball in the box, capitalising on a defensive lapse by Tottenham’s goalkeeper Hugo Lloris. Tottenham responded before half-time, with Son Heung-min equalising in the 36th minute when his header from a corner was met by a composed finish, giving the hosts an early advantage. In the 51st minute, Harry Kane rose highest to head home a corner, giving Tottenham an early lead and turning the match in their favour. ￼

2022_wc_poland_argentina:
Poland 1–1 Argentina
2022 FIFA World Cup – Round of 16, Khalifa International Stadium, Doha – 6 June 2022
Poland and Argentina played out a goalless draw in their final group match at the 2022 World Cup, with both teams hoping to progress to the quarter-finals. The match was competitive from the start, with Poland controlling possession and forcing Argentina to defend deep into their own half. The deadlock was broken in the 74th minute, when Alexis McAllister headed in from close range after a well-worked attacking move, but the goal was ruled out for offside. Argentina pushed for an equaliser after the break, but were unable to find a way past the Polish defence, with the goalkeeper making key interventions to keep the scoreline level.
1983_facup_final_brighton_manchesterunit:
Brighton & Hove Albion 1–0 Manchester United
2022–23 Premier League, Wembley Stadium, London – 21 December 2022

Manchester United took the lead in the 23rd minute when Scott Scott finished from close range after a well-worked move inside the Brighton half. Brighton responded in the 39th minute when Alan Davis converted a penalty after a foul in the penalty area, but the goal was ruled out for offside. ￼

After the break, Brighton continued to press for an equaliser but were unable to find a way past Manchester United’s organised defence. The match ended 1–1, with Manchester United winning 4–3 on penalties following a penalty shoot-out.
2024_euro_england_slovakia:
Slovakia 1–1 England
2022 FIFA World Cup – Group G, Al Bayt Stadium, Al Khor – 13 June 2022

England and Slovakia played out a goalless draw in their round-of-16 match at the 2024 European Championships, with neither side able to break the deadlock before half-time. The decisive moment came in the 65th minute, when Martin Dubravka headed in from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving Slovakia the lead. After the break, England’s pressure grew and they had opportunities to extend their lead, but they were unable to find a way through the Slovakian defence. The deadlock was broken in the 83rd minute when Jordan Walker headed in in close range from a Kieran Trippier cross, giving England a lifeline. The match finished 0–0, with both teams sharing the points in their opening Group E match.
2026_ucl_athleticclub_arsenal:
Arsenal 1–0 Athletic Bilbao
2022–23 UEFA Champions League – Round of 16, Emirates Stadium, London – 6 September 2022
Arsenal opened their Champions League campaign with a hard-fought away win over Athletic Bill Bauer in a competitive match at the Emirates Stadium. The match was closely contested from the start, with Athletic controlling possession and pressing Arsenal’s defence early on. The deadlock was broken in the 428th minute, when Ilkay Gündogan headed in from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving the hosts the lead. ￼

In the 63rd minute, Adama Boyro struck a free kick with his left foot to give Athletic the lead after a foul in the box. Arsenal responded quickly, with Inaki Williams converting a penalty in the 7th minute to equalise, but the match remained 0–0 through 90 minutes. Key events:

2022_wc_england_senegal:
Senegal 1–1 England
2022 FIFA World Cup – Round of 16, Al Bayt Stadium, Al Khor – 13 June 2022

England and Senegal played out a goalless draw in their last 16 match at the 2022 FIFA World Cups, with both teams sharing the points in a low-scoring encounter. The opening goal came in the 23rd minute, when Sadio Mané rose highest to head home from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving Senegal an early lead. England responded quickly, with Harry Kane and Raheem Sterling pressing forward and creating opportunities, but Senegal’s defence remained organised and disciplined, and their pressure was limited to limited opportunities. ￼

The match ended 1–0 in favour of Senegal, leaving England with no points from their opening group match.
2021_euro_czechrepublic_england:
England 2–0 Czech Republic
2022 UEFA Euro 2020 – Group A, Group B, Allianz Riviera Stadium, Paris – 23 June 2022

England secured a narrow victory over the Czech Republic in their final group match at Euro 2020, securing their place in the knockout stages with a 2-0 victory. The opening goal came in the 10th minute, when Raheem Sterling rose highest to head home from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving England an early lead. The Czechs responded before half-time, with Bohuslav Klose forcing several saves from England’s goalkeeper Fraser Forster, including a strong save from Sterling, but were unable to find an equaliser. England continued to push forward after the break, with Harry Kane linking up with Sterling and Sadio Mané on the counter-attack, but the Czechs’ defence remained organised and organised, with goalkeeper Thomas Fasnick making key saves to preserve a 1–0 win for the hosts. The match finished 0–0, leaving England in second place in Group A and Czech Republic top of the group on goal difference.
